creator: ngie@FreeBSD.org
text:
I tried using pipx on a recently built version of 15.0-CURRENT and ran into the following issue:
```
$ pipx ensurepath
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/pipx", line 5, in <module>
    from pipx.main import cli
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/pipx/main.py", line 31, in <module>
    from pipx.version import __version__
ImportError: cannot import name '__version__' from 'pipx.version' (/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/pipx/version.py)
$ python3.9 -m pip --version 
pip 23.3.2 from /usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/pip (python 3.9)
$ python3.9 -c 'import setuptools; print(setuptools.__version__)'
63.1.0
$ pkg query "%n %v" py39-pip py39-setuptools py39-pipx
py39-pip 23.3.2
py39-setuptools 63.1.0_1
py39-pipx 1.3.3
$ uname -a
FreeBSD fbsd-current.envy.local 15.0-CURRENT FreeBSD 15.0-CURRENT #4 main-n268212-bd45bbe440f1: Mon Feb 12 18:19:05 PST 2024     ngie@fbsd-current.envy.local:/usr/obj/usr/src/amd64.amd64/sys/GENERIC-NODEBUG amd64
```

This also repros on a 13.2-RELEASE-p4 host:
```
$ pipx --version
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/usr/local/bin/pipx", line 5, in <module>
    from pipx.main import cli
  File "/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/pipx/main.py", line 31, in <module>
    from pipx.version import __version__
ImportError: cannot import name '__version__' from 'pipx.version' (/usr/local/lib/python3.9/site-packages/pipx/version.py)
$ pkg query "%n %v" py39-pip py39-setuptools py39-pipx
py39-pip 23.3.2
py39-setuptools 63.1.0_1
py39-pipx 1.3.3
$ uname -a
FreeBSD gilot.local 13.2-RELEASE-p4 FreeBSD 13.2-RELEASE-p4 GENERIC amd64
```
